vulnerability.title: Verify User Who Owns /var/log Directory
vulnerability.description: To properly set the owner of /var/log, run the command: $ sudo chown root /var/log
vulnerability.recommendation: Remediation script: newown="" if id "0" >/dev/null 2>&1; then newown="0" fi if [[ -z "$newown" ]]; then >&2 echo "0 is not a defined user on the system" else find -P /var/log/ -maxdepth 0 -type d ! -user 0 -exec chown --no-dereference "$newown" {} \; fi

triage_decision.reason: Changing the owner of /var/log to root is a standard file permission remediation with no impact on authentication, authorization, or critical services, and does not require a reboot.
triage_decision.estimated_impact: filesystems affected; Incorrect current ownership may affect log rotation or audit collection

previous_attempts.scan_output: Vulnerability openscap_048 not found in single-rule scan (possibly fixed)

llm_verdict.message: The owner of **/var/log** is already `root`, which satisfies the `file_owner_var_log` rule. No remediation action is required.
